title: UK Department for Culture, Media & Sport – Shaping AI Policy Through Business Adoption Modelling
gist: Capital Economics built a bespoke survey-plus-spreadsheet model of current and projected AI adoption by UK businesses for DCMS, producing the evidence base that underpins UK government AI policy and highlighting market potential, adoption barriers, and policy levers.

insights.claim: Data on AI adoption by UK businesses was fragmented and inconsistent, so the UK government lacked a comprehensive quantitative view of current trends and future potential before commissioning bespoke modelling.
insights.speaker: Capital Economics
insights.so_what: Signals that official AI-adoption baselines were thin as of the engagement, so early proprietary datasets on business AI uptake carry outsized informational value.
insights.quote: With fragmented and inconsistent data on AI adoption, DCMS required a comprehensive, quantitative view of current trends and future potential.

insights.claim: A credible view of national AI demand was built by combining a literature review, official statistics and prior surveys, expert consultation, and a bespoke new business survey into a custom spreadsheet model quantifying current and projected AI demand across UK businesses.
insights.speaker: Capital Economics
insights.so_what: Shows the practical toolkit for sizing AI adoption when clean data is absent; investors sizing AI TAM can mirror this triangulation approach.
insights.quote: These findings were used to build a custom spreadsheet model, quantifying current and projected AI demand across UK businesses.

insights.claim: Capital Economics' AI adoption report was published on GOV.UK and served as a foundation for the UK Department for Culture, Media & Sport's AI policy strategy.
insights.speaker: Capital Economics
insights.so_what: Indicates the underlying report is a public, authoritative primary source for anyone tracking UK AI policy direction.
insights.quote: The report served as a foundation for DCMS’s AI policy strategy, offering an authoritative view of the AI landscape in UK business.

insights.claim: The modelling explicitly identified AI adoption barriers facing UK businesses and where policy could support uptake across industries, framing government intervention as an adoption enabler.
insights.speaker: Capital Economics
insights.so_what: Flags that UK AI policy is oriented toward removing business adoption frictions, a tailwind for AI enablement and services vendors.
